explainScore.text: Your academic performance appears to be supported by a strong foundation of planning and reflection. You are actively setting and adjusting personal learning goals and frequently reviewing system-generated feedback. This shows a clear intention to improve. The next step is to translate this planning into a broader range of consistent practice, as current evidence shows limited engagement in activities like voice practice, which may be a key factor in developing practical skills.

stageJudgements.rationale: You demonstrate a proactive and goal-oriented approach to learning. Setting multiple goals focused on 'Learning Attitude' and making several adjustments indicates a commitment to self-improvement. This is further supported by the high frequency of reviewing feedback, suggesting a reflective mindset.

You are using key learning methods like goal-setting and feedback review. However, the variety of learning activities appears limited, with minimal engagement in voice practice. While reviewing the AI's speech is helpful, the absence of self-replay suggests an opportunity to develop self-monitoring skills further. The connection between planning and active practice could be strengthened to accelerate progress towards your goals.

There is insufficient evidence from practice activities, such as voice exercises or assignments, to provide a meaningful interpretation of this dimension. Engaging in more performance-based tasks would provide the necessary information.
Insufficient evidence. The available data does not contain information related to this dimension.

formativeSuggestions.title: Connect Your Goals to Daily Practice
formativeSuggestions.description: You have set clear goals, which is an excellent first step. To make progress, it's effective to break down these big goals into small, consistent practice habits. This cognitive science principle, known as 'implementation intention', links a goal to a specific action plan, increasing the likelihood of follow-through.
nextActions: Choose one of your 'Learning Attitude' goals.
Identify a specific 10-minute activity (like a voice practice) you can do this week to work towards it.
Schedule this activity in your calendar.

formativeSuggestions.title: Activate Self-Monitoring by Reviewing Your Own Voice
formativeSuggestions.description: You are reviewing the AI model's speech, which is useful for listening practice. A powerful next step is to also listen back to your own voice recordings. This practice of self-monitoring allows you to become your own coach, identifying areas for improvement in pronunciation, fluency, or word choice that you might otherwise miss.
nextActions: After your next voice practice, play back your own audio.
Listen for one aspect you are proud of.
Listen for one specific sound or phrase you want to refine next time.

riskAlerts.title: Potential Gap Between Planning and Action
riskAlerts.message: There is consistent evidence of planning (goal-setting) and reflection (feedback review), but a low volume of corresponding practice. This imbalance could lead to slow progress and potential frustration, as plans and goals are not yet being supported by sufficient skill-building activities.
